description: Work the word "gazpacho" into a conversation naturally.
Use the phrase "statistically speaking" in a sentence without anyone prompting you.
Casually mention a fictional small town called "Millbrook" as if it's a real place you've been.
Say the word "luminous" while describing something — anything.
Work the phrase "back in the day" into a story you tell.
Mention a made-up celebrity named "Derek Voss" as if everyone should know who that is.
Use the word "allegedly" in a sentence about something completely mundane.
Move a throw pillow to a completely different piece of furniture.
Rotate one item on a table or shelf so it faces a different direction.
Leave a random object (like a spoon or a remote) somewhere it clearly doesn't belong.
Flip a small decorative item upside down somewhere subtle.
Move an item from one room to another without anyone noticing.
Stack something on top of something else that it clearly doesn't belong on.
Get someone to count something out loud.
Get two people to high-five without suggesting it directly.
Convince someone to sit somewhere different than their usual spot.
Make someone laugh without being the one who told a joke.
Get someone to check their phone within 10 minutes of the round starting.
Get someone else to bring you a drink without directly asking for one.
Get someone to start a sentence with "One time..."
Get the group to agree to do something (a walk, a game, a snack run) that wasn't already planned.
Make two separate people mention the same topic (e.g. the weather, a movie, food) within the hour without connecting them.
